evidence_items.content_summary: Strategic Intelligence Platform — Evidence-Backed Strategic Intelligence for Enterprise and Public-Sector Decision Teams. A governed strategic-intelligence pilot platform that connects evidence-gathering, game-theory analysis, human review, and forecast governance into one workflow.
evidence_items.claim: Strategic Intelligence Platform — Evidence-Backed Strategic Intelligence for Enterprise and Public-Sector Decision Teams. A governed strategic-intelligence pilot platform that connects evidence-gathering, game-theory analysis, human review, and forecast governance into one workflow.

evidence_items.provenance: direct from positioning document (April Dunford framework)
evidence_items.content_summary: For enterprise strategy, geopolitical risk, and public-sector foresight teams who struggle to turn complex uncertainties into defensible, reviewable decision options, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a governed strategic-intelligence pilot tool that connects evidence-gathering, game-theory analysis, human review, and forecast governance into one workflow — unlike Recorded Future, Palantir AIP, and Metaculus/Good Judgment.
evidence_items.claim: For enterprise strategy, geopolitical risk, and public-sector foresight teams who struggle to turn complex uncertainties into defensible, reviewable decision options, Strategic Intelligence Platform is a governed strategic-intelligence pilot tool that connects evidence-gathering, game-theory analysis, human review, and forecast governance into one workflow — unlike Recorded Future, Palantir AIP, and Metaculus/Good Judgment.

evidence_items.content_summary: Pilot Preview — No Payment Required. Tiers: Free $0, Pro $19, Elite $49, Enterprise $199, Academic $34. Company: Ignite IT Serve.
evidence_items.claim: Pilot Preview — No Payment Required. Tiers: Free $0, Pro $19, Elite $49, Enterprise $199, Academic $34. Company: Ignite IT Serve.

evidence_items.content_summary: Competitive positioning vs Recorded Future ($50K-$150K+/yr), Palantir AIP ($100K+/yr), Metaculus/Good Judgment. Wedge: 'Keep those tools. We handle the workflow after intake.' Honest about weaknesses: no hosted proof, no enterprise certification, 0 customers.
evidence_items.claim: Competitive positioning vs Recorded Future ($50K-$150K+/yr), Palantir AIP ($100K+/yr), Metaculus/Good Judgment. Wedge: 'Keep those tools. We handle the workflow after intake.' Honest about weaknesses: no hosted proof, no enterprise certification, 0 customers.

evidence_items.content_summary: React 18 + Vite 6 + TypeScript 5.6 + Tailwind 3.4 + shadcn/ui (Radix primitives). Supabase backend. Stripe + Whop monetization. Playwright E2E. Vitest unit tests. 91 dependencies total.
evidence_items.claim: React 18 + Vite 6 + TypeScript 5.6 + Tailwind 3.4 + shadcn/ui (Radix primitives). Supabase backend. Stripe + Whop monetization. Playwright E2E. Vitest unit tests. 91 dependencies total.

evidence_items.content_summary: 14 routes: /console (main), /insights (geopolitical), /commodities (gold/market), /labs (practice modules), /templates, /forecasts, /forecasts/new, /classrooms, /war-room, /pricing, /signup, /checkout/stripe, /reviews, /system, /demo, /terms, /privacy. Public beta mode restricts many routes to anonymous analysis only.
evidence_items.claim: 14 routes: /console (main), /insights (geopolitical), /commodities (gold/market), /labs (practice modules), /templates, /forecasts, /forecasts/new, /classrooms, /war-room, /pricing, /signup, /checkout/stripe, /reviews, /system, /demo, /terms, /privacy. Public beta mode restricts many routes to anonymous analysis only.

evidence_items.claim: 56 React components including: StrategyConsole (99KB — main analysis interface), ForecastRegistry (51KB — forecast governance), MultiAgentForecastPanel (43KB), StrategySimulator (42KB), GameTreeBuilder (26KB), ClassroomManager (26KB), NegotiationDojo (25KB), GoldGameModule (24KB), CorporateWarRoom (23KB), BiasProfileDashboard (21KB), HumanReview (21KB), ScenarioMarketplace (20KB), GeopoliticalDashboard (18KB), WhopPricingPage (26KB), and more.

evidence_items.content_summary: 55 database tables across: core game theory (10), real-time/historical (5), collective intelligence (8), additional modules (8), multi-user/signaling (5), supporting infrastructure (19). RLS enabled on all tables.

evidence_items.content_summary: Claim governance system with ALLOWED_CLAIMS (9) and PROHIBITED_CLAIMS (14). Prohibited: 'world-class accurate predictions', 'enterprise-ready platform', 'SOC 2 certified', 'buyer-validated willingness to pay', 'Palantir-equivalent'. This shows the team is honest about limitations.

Prohibited: 'world-class accurate predictions', 'enterprise-ready platform', 'SOC 2 certified', 'buyer-validated willingness to pay', 'Palantir-equivalent'. evidence_items.claim: Forecast governance implemented: readiness assessment (issues/warnings/score), publish governance (canPublish/blockers/reviewRequired/warnings/freshness), pre-resolution capture, Brier scoring awareness.

evidence_items.content_summary: 5 pricing tiers: Free ($0, 5 analyses/day, 2x2 matrices), Pro ($19, 50/day, 5x5, labs, negotiation dojo, bias profile), Elite ($49, unlimited, gold module, intel, trading signals, war room, API), Enterprise ($199, white-label, SSO, SLA, 10 seats), Academic ($34, .edu only, classroom mode, LMS support). Whop primary + Stripe fallback for .edu.

Whop primary + Stripe fallback for .edu.

evidence_items.content_summary: PostHog analytics integration (optional, env-gated). Events: page_view, signup_start, signup_complete, analysis_run, pricing_view, pricing_pilot_request, demo_request, forecast_create, forecast_publish. No Sentry error tracking active (env var empty in .env.example).
evidence_items.claim: PostHog analytics integration (optional, env-gated). Events: page_view, signup_start, signup_complete, analysis_run, pricing_view, pricing_pilot_request, demo_request, forecast_create, forecast_publish. No Sentry error tracking active (env var empty in .env.example).

evidence_items.content_summary: Integrations: Supabase, Gemini (primary LLM), OpenRouter (fallback), OpenAI, xAI (Grok), Perplexity (evidence retrieval), Firecrawl (web scraping), GDELT (geopolitical events), World Bank, Coinbase (BTC price), Stripe, Whop, PostHog, Sentry (disabled). Public beta mode: VITE_PUBLIC_BETA_MODE=analysis_only.
evidence_items.claim: Integrations: Supabase, Gemini (primary LLM), OpenRouter (fallback), OpenAI, xAI (Grok), Perplexity (evidence retrieval), Firecrawl (web scraping), GDELT (geopolitical events), World Bank, Coinbase (BTC price), Stripe, Whop, PostHog, Sentry (disabled). evidence_items.content_summary: Public beta mode (VITE_PUBLIC_BETA_MODE=analysis_only) restricts: insights, labs, classrooms, war-room, pricing, signup, checkout, forecasts/new, reviews, academic-application. Only /console, /templates, /forecasts (read), /demo are publicly accessible. This means the product is in a restricted public beta — not fully launched.
evidence_items.claim: Public beta mode (VITE_PUBLIC_BETA_MODE=analysis_only) restricts: insights, labs, classrooms, war-room, pricing, signup, checkout, forecasts/new, reviews, academic-application. Only /console, /templates, /forecasts (read), /demo are publicly accessible. This means the product is in a restricted public beta — not fully launched.

evidence_items.content_summary: App.tsx imports from './lib/labsCatalog', './lib/accessOverrides', './lib/publicBeta' — but these files do NOT exist in src/lib/ (only 9 files present: analytics, claimRegistry, forecastGovernance, llm-prompts, localEngine, supabase, utils, whop-config, whop). This suggests either: (a) files are generated at build time, (b) files are missing and build would fail, or (c) there's a barrel export not visible. This is a codebase health finding.
evidence_items.claim: App.tsx imports from './lib/labsCatalog', './lib/accessOverrides', './lib/publicBeta' — but these files do NOT exist in src/lib/ (only 9 files present: analytics, claimRegistry, forecastGovernance, llm-prompts, localEngine, supabase, utils, whop-config, whop). This suggests either: (a) files are generated at build time, (b) files are missing and build would fail, or (c) there's a barrel export not visible. This is a codebase health finding.

evidence_items.content_summary: Explicit 'What We Don't Claim' section: No world-class prediction accuracy, no enterprise-ready security (0/8 procurement documents), no buyer-validated WTP (0 completed calls), no hosted/live proof, no Palantir-equivalence. Dual positioning: enterprise leads, consumer features secondary.

evidence_items.content_summary: Monetization strategy positions platform at intersection of EdTech, FinTech, and Strategic Intelligence. Core value prop: 'democratize Structural Forecasting' — game theory models future based on rational/irrational incentives, not just time-series. Target: researchers, educators, enterprise SaaS.

Core value prop: 'democratize Structural Forecasting' — game theory models future based on rational/irrational incentives, not just time-series. Target: researchers, educators, enterprise SaaS.

evidence_items.content_summary: 5 competition-winning innovations: (1) AI Personal Strategic Life Coach, (2) Global Cooperation Engine, (3) Matching Markets (Nobel Prize algorithms), (4) Strategic DNA (25-bias assessment), (5) AI Conflict Mediator. These are consumer/education-facing features, not enterprise.
evidence_items.claim: 5 competition-winning innovations: (1) AI Personal Strategic Life Coach, (2) Global Cooperation Engine, (3) Matching Markets (Nobel Prize algorithms), (4) Strategic DNA (25-bias assessment), (5) AI Conflict Mediator. These are consumer/education-facing features, not enterprise.

Overall: core functional, payments and some feeds need configuration.

evidence_items.content_summary: Windows build failure fixed: replaced Unix-only 'rm -rf node_modules/.vite-temp' with cross-platform Node.js fs.rmSync via 'clean' script. Build and build:prod now use 'pnpm clean && tsc -b && vite build'.
evidence_items.claim: Windows build failure fixed: replaced Unix-only 'rm -rf node_modules/.vite-temp' with cross-platform Node.js fs.rmSync via 'clean' script. Build and build:prod now use 'pnpm clean && tsc -b && vite build'.

evidence_items.content_summary: Assignment creation now implemented: 'New Assignment' button wired to showAssignmentForm state, handleCreateAssignment function inserts into classroom_assignments table with title/description/due_date/points_possible. Form UI added with input fields.
evidence_items.claim: Assignment creation now implemented: 'New Assignment' button wired to showAssignmentForm state, handleCreateAssignment function inserts into classroom_assignments table with title/description/due_date/points_possible. Form UI added with input fields.

evidence_items.source: supabase/functions/lti-launch/index.ts (PARTIALLY_FIXED — JWT verification fixed but JWKS uses placeholder RSA modulus)
evidence_items.content_summary: LTI JWT security partially fixed: replaced insecure decodeJWT (no signature verification) with verifyJWT using Web Crypto API. Now fetches JWKS from platform's jwks_url, validates issuer/audience/expiration, and verifies RS256 signature. Returns 401 on verification failure. HOWEVER, JWKS still uses placeholder RSA modulus (n: 'placeholder') — not production-verified. LTI 1.3/OIDC launch, signature validation, and Canvas/Moodle sandbox not tested. Status: blocked_partial.
evidence_items.claim: LTI JWT security fixed: replaced insecure decodeJWT (no signature verification) with verifyJWT using Web Crypto API. Now fetches JWKS from platform's jwks_url, validates issuer/audience/expiration, and verifies RS256 signature. Returns 401 on verification failure.

evidence_items.content_summary: DB permissions fixed: added INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE grants on classrooms, classroom_members, classroom_assignments, and assignment_submissions tables for authenticated users. Previously only SELECT was granted, preventing assignment creation.
evidence_items.claim: DB permissions fixed: added INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE grants on classrooms, classroom_members, classroom_assignments, and assignment_submissions tables for authenticated users. Previously only SELECT was granted, preventing assignment creation.

evidence_items.content_summary: MobLab Classroom: 60+ games and simulations for economics, social science, business, strategic thinking. Features include: online assignments (quizzes, video instructions, games, reflection surveys), expert-built Modules or build-your-own, in-class playlists (synchronous) and online assignments (asynchronous). NOT 'experiments only' as previously stated.
evidence_items.claim: MobLab Classroom: 60+ games and simulations for economics, social science, business, strategic thinking. Features include: online assignments (quizzes, video instructions, games, reflection surveys), expert-built Modules or build-your-own, in-class playlists (synchronous) and online assignments (asynchronous). NOT 'experiments only' as previously stated.

evidence_items.content_summary: MobLab pricing: DayPass (per student/day), Premier $25/student/class (60+ games), Poll+Games $15/student/class, Poll $12/student/class. Free for classes <8 students. High school 50% discount. Bundled with Macmillan, McGraw-Hill, Cengage/MindTap textbooks. Instructor access free.
evidence_items.claim: MobLab pricing: DayPass (per student/day), Premier $25/student/class (60+ games), Poll+Games $15/student/class, Poll $12/student/class. Free for classes <8 students. High school 50% discount. Bundled with Macmillan, McGraw-Hill, Cengage/MindTap textbooks. Instructor access free.

evidence_items.provenance: direct from MobLab game theory page
evidence_items.content_summary: MobLab game theory catalog: Prisoner's Dilemma, Beauty Contest, Public Good, Cournot, Two-Candidate Election, Bach/Stravinsky, Stag Hunt, Matching Pennies, Rock-Paper-Scissors, Bargaining, Trust Game, Stackelberg, Monty Hall, Market for Lemons, Auctions, Ultimatum, Dictator. All can be repeated indefinitely.
evidence_items.claim: MobLab game theory catalog: Prisoner's Dilemma, Beauty Contest, Public Good, Cournot, Two-Candidate Election, Bach/Stravinsky, Stag Hunt, Matching Pennies, Rock-Paper-Scissors, Bargaining, Trust Game, Stackelberg, Monty Hall, Market for Lemons, Auctions, Ultimatum, Dictator. All can be repeated indefinitely.

evidence_items.content_summary: MobLab LMS integration: built-in gradebook and roster syncs with Blackboard and Canvas. Yale SOM uses MobLab at $12/student. Harvard Business Publishing distributes MobLab simulations (prices $5-$60 per simulation). Cengage partnership includes MobLab in MindTap.
evidence_items.claim: MobLab LMS integration: built-in gradebook and roster syncs with Blackboard and Canvas. Yale SOM uses MobLab at $12/student. Harvard Business Publishing distributes MobLab simulations (prices $5-$60 per simulation). Cengage partnership includes MobLab in MindTap.

evidence_items.content_summary: Wolfram Language 14.2 (Feb 2025) added built-in game theory functions: MatrixGame, TreeGame, NashEquilibrium, DominantStrategies, and game theory data. Targets researchers and students. Part of Mathematica ($184/student/year, $3K+ for institutions). Not a classroom management tool.
evidence_items.claim: Wolfram Language 14.2 (Feb 2025) added built-in game theory functions: MatrixGame, TreeGame, NashEquilibrium, DominantStrategies, and game theory data. Targets researchers and students. Part of Mathematica ($184/student/year, $3K+ for institutions). Not a classroom management tool.

evidence_items.content_summary: Gambit: open-source game theory computation tool. Graphical interface for building/analyzing games, command-line tools, Python API. Used in teaching and research. Free. Alan Turing Institute developing AI-powered Gambit for non-experts. No classroom management, no LMS integration, no evidence retrieval.
evidence_items.claim: Gambit: open-source game theory computation tool. Graphical interface for building/analyzing games, command-line tools, Python API. Used in teaching and research. Free. Alan Turing Institute developing AI-powered Gambit for non-experts. No classroom management, no LMS integration, no evidence retrieval.

evidence_items.content_summary: Coursera offers Stanford/UBC Game Theory I&II (Jackson, Leyton-Brown, Shoham). Yale OpenCourseWare Econ-159 (Polak). eCornell Game Theory and Business Strategy. These are content-only, not interactive analysis tools. Free to ~$49/month subscription.
evidence_items.claim: Coursera offers Stanford/UBC Game Theory I&II (Jackson, Leyton-Brown, Shoham). Yale OpenCourseWare Econ-159 (Polak). eCornell Game Theory and Business Strategy. These are content-only, not interactive analysis tools. Free to ~$49/month subscription.

evidence_items.content_summary: Dartmouth game theory enrollment: 248 students in Evolutionary Game Theory (spring 2023), up from 9 in 2016. 300+ undergraduates study game theory yearly across 3 courses. Professor Fu: 'We're facing pandemics, AI ethics — game theory helps find common ground.' QSS program growth driving demand.
evidence_items.claim: Dartmouth game theory enrollment: 248 students in Evolutionary Game Theory (spring 2023), up from 9 in 2016. 300+ undergraduates study game theory yearly across 3 courses. Professor Fu: 'We're facing pandemics, AI ethics — game theory helps find common ground.' QSS program growth driving demand.

evidence_items.content_summary: Global education software market: $27.8B (2025) → $30.67B (2026), 10.28% CAGR. Higher education ~25% of market. AI in education $5.88B (2024) → $32.27B (2030), 31.2% CAGR. LMS = 46.35% of ed software spend. Gamification improves completion +34%, retention +22%.
evidence_items.claim: Global education software market: $27.8B (2025) → $30.67B (2026), 10.28% CAGR. Higher education ~25% of market. AI in education $5.88B (2024) → $32.27B (2030), 31.2% CAGR. LMS = 46.35% of ed software spend. Gamification improves completion +34%, retention +22%.

evidence_items.content_summary: Global EdTech market: $199.74B (2025) → $236.25B (2026), 18.3% CAGR → $456.41B by 2030. 47% of HE institutions use AI structurally. 72% of educators experimented with generative AI. Adaptive learning improves outcomes +23%. LMS market alone: $30.92B (2025) → $100.70B (2032).
evidence_items.claim: Global EdTech market: $199.74B (2025) → $236.25B (2026), 18.3% CAGR → $456.41B by 2030. 47% of HE institutions use AI structurally. 72% of educators experimented with generative AI. Adaptive learning improves outcomes +23%. LMS market alone: $30.92B (2025) → $100.70B (2032).

evidence_items.content_summary: Education gamification market: $10.37B (2025) → $35.65B (2035), 13.14% CAGR. Game-based learning growing at 21.6% CAGR. 88% of teachers using digital games report increased student engagement.
evidence_items.claim: Education gamification market: $10.37B (2025) → $35.65B (2035), 13.14% CAGR. Game-based learning growing at 21.6% CAGR. 88% of teachers using digital games report increased student engagement.

evidence_items.content_summary: AI chatbot substitution in education: Generative AI compromising higher education non-exam assessments. AI detection insufficient. Frontiers in Education (2026): need task-specific AI rules distinguishing support from substitution. 47% of HE institutions use AI structurally. This validates need for AI-resistant, workflow-based tools.
evidence_items.claim: AI chatbot substitution in education: Generative AI compromising higher education non-exam assessments. AI detection insufficient. Frontiers in Education (2026): need task-specific AI rules distinguishing support from substitution. 47% of HE institutions use AI structurally. This validates need for AI-resistant, workflow-based tools.

evidence_items.content_summary: ClassroomManager (26KB, 748 lines): manages classrooms (create/join), members (invite/remove), assignments (now create with form), classroom settings. Roles: instructor, TA, student, observer. Join codes. BUT: no gradebook, no LMS sync (LTI exists but unverified), no quiz/test creation, no student progress tracking dashboard.
evidence_items.claim: ClassroomManager (26KB, 748 lines): manages classrooms (create/join), members (invite/remove), assignments (now create with form), classroom settings. Roles: instructor, TA, student, observer. Join codes. BUT: no gradebook, no LMS sync (LTI exists but unverified), no quiz/test creation, no student progress tracking dashboard.

evidence_items.content_summary: NegotiationDojo (25KB): AI-powered negotiation practice with game-theoretic opponents. Scenario-based. Tracks outcomes and strategic ratings. Standalone lab module, not integrated with classroom assignments or grading.
evidence_items.claim: NegotiationDojo (25KB): AI-powered negotiation practice with game-theoretic opponents. Scenario-based. Tracks outcomes and strategic ratings. Standalone lab module, not integrated with classroom assignments or grading.

evidence_items.content_summary: TeacherView: renders lesson outlines, grading rubrics, student handouts from analysis data. Read-only view — no assignment creation, no grade input, no student feedback mechanism.
evidence_items.claim: TeacherView: renders lesson outlines, grading rubrics, student handouts from analysis data. Read-only view — no assignment creation, no grade input, no student feedback mechanism.

evidence_items.content_summary: Teacher-packet edge function: generates lesson plans, learning objectives, handouts from analysis data. One-way generation — no feedback loop, no student submission handling.
evidence_items.claim: Teacher-packet edge function: generates lesson plans, learning objectives, handouts from analysis data. One-way generation — no feedback loop, no student submission handling.

evidence_items.content_summary: LTI 1.3 launch handler: NOW fixed with JWKS signature verification. Previously decoded JWTs without verification (security vulnerability). Still requires: platform registration flow testing, JWKS URL configuration in lti_platforms table, end-to-end Canvas/Moodle test.
evidence_items.claim: LTI 1.3 launch handler: NOW fixed with JWKS signature verification. Previously decoded JWTs without verification (security vulnerability). Still requires: platform registration flow testing, JWKS URL configuration in lti_platforms table, end-to-end Canvas/Moodle test.

evidence_items.content_summary: Academic tier: $34/month with .edu email verification. Features: classroom mode, LMS support, unlimited analyses, 10x10 matrices. Whop primary billing, Stripe fallback for .edu. BUT: no per-student pricing model (MobLab charges $12-$25/student), no institutional license option.
evidence_items.claim: Academic tier: $34/month with .edu email verification. Features: classroom mode, LMS support, unlimited analyses, 10x10 matrices. Whop primary billing, Stripe fallback for .edu. BUT: no per-student pricing model (MobLab charges $12-$25/student), no institutional license option.

evidence_items.content_summary: MobLab has: 60+ games, assignments, quizzes, gradebook, Canvas/Blackboard sync, textbook bundling, 10+ years market presence, Yale/Harvard adoption. SIP has: Nash equilibrium computation, evidence retrieval, forecast governance, classroom management, negotiation dojo, bias profile, scenario marketplace. SIP unique: evidence-backed analysis + forecast governance + real-world scenario analysis. MobLab unique: 60+ interactive games, gradebook, LMS sync, textbook partnerships.
evidence_items.claim: MobLab has: 60+ games, assignments, quizzes, gradebook, Canvas/Blackboard sync, textbook bundling, 10+ years market presence, Yale/Harvard adoption. SIP has: Nash equilibrium computation, evidence retrieval, forecast governance, classroom management, negotiation dojo, bias profile, scenario marketplace. SIP unique: evidence-backed analysis + forecast governance + real-world scenario analysis. MobLab unique: 60+ interactive games, gradebook, LMS sync, textbook partnerships.

evidence_items.content_summary: MIT offers Economic Applications of Game Theory (14.12) with instructor insights on teaching. Validates real teaching need. One course does not prove market growth or WTP. Need broader survey of game theory course offerings.
evidence_items.claim: MIT offers Economic Applications of Game Theory (14.12) with instructor insights on teaching. Validates real teaching need. One course does not prove market growth or WTP. Need broader survey of game theory course offerings.

evidence_items.content_summary: HBP MobLab simulation prices effective July 2026: degree courses $5-$25, non-degree $10-$60 per simulation. Shows per-simulation pricing model and institutional distribution channel via HBP.
evidence_items.claim: HBP MobLab simulation prices effective July 2026: degree courses $5-$25, non-degree $10-$60 per simulation. Shows per-simulation pricing model and institutional distribution channel via HBP.

evidence_items.claim: Yale School of Management uses MobLab at $12/student. Integrated with Canvas. Includes Strategy, Game Theory, Principles of Economics, Macroeconomics. Shows institutional adoption at top-tier university.

evidence_items.content_summary: shared/ contains 10 modules: agentOrchestrator, benchmarkRegistry, confidenceCalibration, evidenceGate, evidenceGraph, forecastJudge, forecastSkills, marketPriors, mlAdvisory, semanticRouter. mlAdvisory has labelCalibrationStatus and normalizeCalibrationStatus. publicForecasting.ts was missing and is now created.
evidence_items.claim: shared/ contains 10 modules: agentOrchestrator, benchmarkRegistry, confidenceCalibration, evidenceGate, evidenceGraph, forecastJudge, forecastSkills, marketPriors, mlAdvisory, semanticRouter. mlAdvisory has labelCalibrationStatus and normalizeCalibrationStatus. publicForecasting.ts was missing and is now created.

evidence_items.content_summary: useStrategyAnalysis hook imports educationHelpers for mode-aware generation: player interactions, decision alternatives, information nodes, current beliefs, outcome scenarios, decay models, external factors. In education mode, returns empty arrays (students determine values themselves). educationHelpers.ts was missing and is now created.

Missing: forecast governance, human review workflow, Brier scoring.

evidence_items.content_summary: KiteEdge: AI Decision Infrastructure. Apex governance layer: pre-retrieval permissions, source allowlists, evidence set visibility, audit logs, approval checkpoints, reproducible re-runs. Evidence-bounded reasoning using only approved knowledge. Re-evaluate decisions as data changes with full audit continuity. Competes on evidence-bounded reasoning + governance. Missing: game theory, forecasting.

evidence_items.content_summary: Qonera: AI review and approval workflow. Checks evidence, flags unsupported claims, surfaces source conflicts, compares independent model outputs. Missing: game theory, forecasting.

evidence_items.content_summary: FinHelm: Probabilistic finance platform for FP&A. Reflection Engine with Bayesian closed-loop calibration. Forecast Fidelity Score (accuracy, bias, volatility, persistence on 0-100 scale). Six months of calibration history not portable backwards. Tiers: visibility, planning, governance. Competes on forecast calibration. Missing: game theory, evidence retrieval, human review. FP&A focused, not strategic intelligence.

Missing: game theory, human review.

evidence_items.content_summary: Global competitive intelligence market projected to reach $16.8B by 2030, more than doubling current size. Key players: Klue, Crayon, Semrush, AlphaSense, Contify, Valona. CI tools collect data that never reaches people who need it. Enterprise software giants (IBM, Oracle, Microsoft) embed CI in broader BI/analytics. Specialized tools addressing specific CI needs expected to grow.

evidence_items.content_summary: Strategic foresight platforms market is fragmented and dynamic, no single player commanding dominant market share. Competition across technological capability, methodological rigor, domain expertise, UX, and implementation ecosystems. Key battlegrounds to 2035: sophistication of embedded AI (descriptive → prescriptive → generative scenarios), ecosystem integrations, proving measurable strategic impact to C-suite. Consolidation likely.

evidence_items.content_summary: AI geopolitical risk analysis tools ranked 2026: Recorded Future (enterprise intelligence, benchmark), Seerist (corporate security), Predata (political signals), GeoQuant (investors/financial, now part of Fitch), Palantir AIP (government/defense, most powerful, months to implement), Crisis24 Horizon (global operations), Blackbird.AI (narrative/disinformation). Only 6 of 15 tools deemed worth it. Recorded Future and Palantir in different category — for organizations with dedicated intelligence teams and significant budgets.

Most CI tools serve sales teams, not strategy teams.
